64	  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
65	  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
66	fi
67	
68	# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
69	# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
70	# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
71	RT_TESTS=(
72	  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
73	  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
74	  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
75	  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
76	  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
77	  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
78	  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
79	  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
80	  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
81	  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
82	  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
83	  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
84	  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
85	  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
86	  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
87	  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
88	  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
89	)
90	
91	NEG_TESTS=(
92	  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
93	  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
94	  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
95	  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
96	)
97	
98	MODE="${MODE:-both}"
99	case "$MODE" in
100	  csr|pc|both) ;;
101	  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
102	esac
103	
104	default_jobs() {
105	  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
106	  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
107	  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
108	    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
109	    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
110	      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
111	      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
112	    fi
113	  fi
114	  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
115	  printf '%d\n' "$n"
116	}
117	JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
118	[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
119	  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run the hybrid e2e tests directly, bypassing the VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID
# CTest gate. Drives hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/*.sh against pre-built
# artifacts in this workspace.
#
# Env overrides:
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64 (default: build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64)
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so (default: qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so)
#   VSIM_BIN     sim_<cpu> (default: first sim_* under hybrid_vsim/build/)
#   FILTER       regex; only run tests whose name matches
#   MODE         csr | pc | both  (default: both) - drives TRIGGER_MODE
#                for round-trip fixtures. Negative drivers run once
#                regardless because they assert orchestrator failure
#                modes orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
#   JOBS         parallel job slots (default: min($(nproc),
#                free(/dev/shm)/256MB)). Each Tier-2 slot uses a 128 MB
#                /dev/shm mmap and runs 3 processes (qemu1, vsim, qemu2);
#                the headroom check leaves margin for that plus QEMU's
#                own scratch. Pass JOBS=1 for sequential semantics.
#   LOG_DIR      where per-test logs land (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
VSIM_DIR="$ROOT/hybrid_vsim"
HARNESS="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"
TEST_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/hybrid/test"

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
if [[ -z "${VSIM_BIN:-}" ]]; then
  VSIM_BIN=$(ls "$VSIM_DIR"/build/sim_* 2>/dev/null | head -n1 || true)
fi

step() { printf '\033[1;34m==>\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
ok()   { printf '\033[1;32m[ok]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '\033[1;33m[skip]\033[0m %s\n' "$*"; }
fail() { printf '\033[1;31m[FAIL]\033[0m %s\n' "$*" >&2; }

require() {
  if [[ -z "$2" || ! -e "$2" ]]; then
    fail "$1 not found: $2"
    fail "build artifacts first: 'source ./setup.sh'"
    exit 1
  fi
}
require VSIM_QEMU   "$VSIM_QEMU"
require VSIM_PLUGIN "$VSIM_PLUGIN"
require VSIM_BIN    "$VSIM_BIN"

export VSIM_QEMU VSIM_PLUGIN VSIM_BIN

REQUIRED_ELFS=(
  handoff_roundtrip rt_pc_jump rt_sign_bits rt_abi_aliases rt_long_roi
  rt_all_gprs rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh rt_csr_survival rt_shared_mem
  rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs
  rt_c_v_matmul
)
need_build=0
for f in "${REQUIRED_ELFS[@]}"; do
  [[ -e "$TEST_DIR/$f.elf" ]] || need_build=1
done
if (( need_build )); then
  step "building test ELFs in $TEST_DIR"
  make -C "$TEST_DIR" -j
fi

# Round-trip and Tier-2 fixtures: name|fixture-env-prefix|harness. The
# TRIGGER_MODE env is injected per iteration based on $MODE. Negative
# drivers live in NEG_TESTS and run once regardless of mode.
RT_TESTS=(
  "handoff_roundtrip|FIXTURE=handoff_roundtrip EXPECT_RC=3|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_pc_jump|FIXTURE=rt_pc_jump EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_sign_bits|FIXTURE=rt_sign_bits EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_abi_aliases|FIXTURE=rt_abi_aliases EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_long_roi|FIXTURE=rt_long_roi EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_all_gprs|FIXTURE=rt_all_gprs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_mem_loadstore|FIXTURE=rt_mem_loadstore EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_unaligned_lh|FIXTURE=rt_unaligned_lh EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_csr_survival|FIXTURE=rt_csr_survival EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
  "rt_shared_mem||$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_hello|FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_printf|FIXTURE=rt_c_printf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_regs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_csrs|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_csrs EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_v_matmul|FIXTURE=rt_c_v_matmul EXPECT_RC=0 TIMEOUT=120|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
  "rt_c_static_buf|FIXTURE=rt_c_static_buf EXPECT_RC=0|$HARNESS/rt_shared_mem.sh"
)

NEG_TESTS=(
  "rt_corrupt_state|bash $HARNESS/rt_corrupt_state.sh"
  "rt_no_qemu_binary|bash $HARNESS/rt_no_qemu_binary.sh"
  "rt_gdbstub_timeout|bash $HARNESS/rt_gdbstub_timeout.sh"
  "rt_pty_handback|bash $HARNESS/rt_pty_handback.sh"
)

MODE="${MODE:-both}"
case "$MODE" in
  csr|pc|both) ;;
  *) fail "MODE must be one of csr|pc|both (got: $MODE)"; exit 1 ;;
esac

default_jobs() {
  local n=1 shm_free shm_cap
  n=$(nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
  if [[ -d /dev/shm ]]; then
    shm_free=$(df -m --output=avail /dev/shm 2>/dev/null | tail -n +2 | tr -d ' ')
    if [[ "$shm_free" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$shm_free" -gt 0 ]]; then
      shm_cap=$((shm_free / 256))
      (( shm_cap < n && shm_cap >= 1 )) && n=$shm_cap
    fi
  fi
  (( n < 1 )) && n=1
  printf '%d\n' "$n"
}
JOBS="${JOBS:-$(default_jobs)}"
[[ "$JOBS" =~ ^[0-9]+$ && "$JOBS" -ge 1 ]] \
  || { fail "JOBS must be a positive integer (got: $JOBS)"; exit 1; }

input.content:
# Comprehensive E2E Test Plan: Switching-Mode Demonstration

## Context

The hybrid simulator now ships **all eight switching mechanisms** (F1-F12) that select when QEMU yields to vsim and vice versa, but the test surface treats them as isolated plugin checks. There is no single artifact a new user (or contributor) can run that demonstrates every switching mode end-to-end, and the SimPoint-driven flow - the highest-leverage mode for production benchmarking - has no integrated test that chains BBV emit -> SimPoint clustering -> phase replay.

This plan adds:
1. One **demonstration e2e fixture per switching mode**, each named after the mode it shows off, runnable via a single `make demo` target.
2. The **SimPoint integrated loop** (currently the only F-feature without a chain-of-modes test): BBV profile -> simpoint binary -> archive each chosen phase -> replay each phase under vsim.
3. A **mode-matrix CTest section** in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` so every switching mode has a labeled CTest entry and shows up under `ctest -L switching-mode`.

Intended outcome: a user can run `make demo` and watch QEMU<->vsim swap drivers eight different ways in under five minutes; a contributor breaking any switching mode gets a labeled CTest failure that names the mode.

---

## Switching Mode Catalog (eight modes, today)

| # | Mode | Trigger source | Today's test |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | **CSR insn match** | `csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1}` in binary | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=csr) |
| M2 | **PC symbol resolve** | `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` ELF symbols | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=pc) |
| M3 | **Icount** (F5/F6) | plugin `icount=N`, vsim `--hybrid-icount N` | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M4 | **BBV-emit** (F7) | plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N` | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` |
| M5 | **Slice-consume** (F11) | plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M6 | **Archive emit** (F9) | orchestrator YAML `on_exit.archive_as` | unit test only |
| M7 | **Checkpoint replay** (F10) | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | unit test only |
| M8 | **End-of-program** | QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit | implicit in every Tier-2 e2e |

Coverage gaps (the targets of this plan):
- **M3** has no vsim-side e2e (vsim receives plugin-drained state and resumes).
- **M5** has no orchestrator-level e2e (drain at slice K -> resume under vsim -> finish).
- **M6+M7** have no e2e at all - both are unit-tested in isolation; never wired through a real round-trip.
- **No fixture chains M4 -> simpoint binary -> M5 -> M6 -> M7** (the SimPoint integrated loop).

---

## New Tests to Add (one per mode)

All seven new e2e drivers live under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` and reuse the existing `icount_spin.elf` workload (CSR-less spin at 0x100000), `rt_c_v_regs.elf` for state-rich modes, plus the orchestrator CLI. Each driver writes its own short log preamble naming the mode it exercises.

### T1. `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (covers M1, M8)
Re-thin wrapper over `roundtrip_e2e.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Justification: a single self-documenting script that prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1)" before invoking the harness. No new code, just a labeled entrypoint for the demo runner.

### T2. `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (covers M2)
Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path runs end-to-end.

### T3. `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M3 end-to-end)
- Drives `icount_spin.elf` through QEMU plugin with `icount=10000`.
- Plugin drains at insn 10000 -> exit 200.
- Orchestrator hands state to vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` (vsim drains 5k cycles later).
- vsim exits 200; spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub; QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit.
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0, drained PC in `icount_spin` body both times.
- Why new: today only `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` exists, and it terminates after QEMU1 drain. The vsim resume path under icount mode is untested at e2e level.

### T4. `demo_bbv_profile.sh` (covers M4 + tool integration)
- Generalize the existing `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` so it leaves the `.bb` file in `$LOG_DIR/bbv/icount_spin.bb` instead of `mktemp`.
- Run `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile bbv/icount_spin.bb -maxK 8 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights weights`.
- Assert the output files exist, parse them, print the chosen slice indices + weights as the demo output.
- Pass criterion: simpoint binary exits 0, weights sum to 1.0 (+/-1e-3).
- No vsim involved; this is the QEMU-only profiling pass.

### T5. `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M5 end-to-end + M8)
- Read the `simpts` file from T4's output (or accept `SLICE_AT=K` env override).
- Drive `icount_spin.elf` with `slice=1000,slice_at=K,icount=<large fallback>`.
- Plugin drains exactly at insn (K+1) * 1000.
- vsim resumes, runs ROI, drains via CSR or icount (configurable), QEMU2 finishes.
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body, QEMU2 exit 0.
- Why new: today `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` is QEMU-only.

### T6. `demo_archive_replay.sh` (NEW; covers M6 + M7 end-to-end)
- Step 1: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root $LOG_DIR/snap --workdir $LOG_DIR/run1`
  - Asserts `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist.
  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.

### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:

```
1. Profile pass (M4)
   $ qemu+plugin icount_spin.elf bbv_out=bbv.bb slice=1000 icount=10000
   -> 10-row bbv.bb

2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)
   $ simpoint -loadFVFile bbv.bb -maxK 4 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights w
   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights

3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
     YAML:
       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

4. For each archive: (M7)
   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
```

Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:
- Each phase archive validates.
- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.

Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).

### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo      # alias for ./demo.sh
```

---

## Mode-Matrix CTest Section

Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:

```cmake
# Switching-mode demo set. Each test asserts one of the eight modes
# (M1..M8) end-to-end. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
# the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`.
set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
  "demo_csr_oneshot:M1+M8:CSR insn match -> end-of-program"
  "demo_pc_oneshot:M2:PC symbol resolve"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip:M3:Icount trigger E2E"
  "demo_bbv_profile:M4:BBV emit + SimPoint clustering"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:M5:Slice-consume drain E2E"
  "demo_archive_replay:M6+M7:Checkpoint archive + replay"
  "demo_simpoint_loop:M4->M5->M6->M7:Full SimPoint pipeline"
)
foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 mode)
  list(GET parts 2 desc)
  add_test(NAME test_${fname}
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${fname}.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
    COST 60)
endforeach()
```

Each test honors `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` so missing simpoint binary / missing plugin / missing ELFs skip cleanly rather than failing the suite on a fresh checkout.

---

## Critical Files

To create (no existing implementation):
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh` - user-facing runner
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` - parameterized SimPoint phase YAML used by T7

To modify:
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach (see above)
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - new `demo` phony target invoking `demo.sh`
- `run_e2e.sh` - extend `RT_TESTS` with the seven demo entries so the host runner picks them up (single `MODE=demo` knob optional)

To reuse without modification:
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - already supports every plugin-side switching arg
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` - F12 clustering binary
- `tools/orchestrator/main.py` + `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` - YAML driver and example
- `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (`write_archive`, `replay_checkpoint`, `validate_replay_meta`) - F9+F10 entry points
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_{bbv_emit,phase_consume,icount_trigger}.sh` - reference implementations for plugin invocation patterns
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/{icount_spin.S,rt_c_v_regs.c}` - workloads
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{handoff_controller,resume_driver,state_drain,qemu_handback}.hpp` - the entire vsim-side switching mechanism

---

## Verification

End-to-end ladder, each step requires the previous:

1. **Build**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` builds all referenced ELFs (already exist; rule is just an alias for `make demo.sh`).
2. **Single-mode**: `bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` exits 0 and prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1) - PASS".
3. **Plugin-only modes**: `bash demo_bbv_profile.sh` produces a `.bb` file, runs simpoint, prints chosen slices + weights.
4. **Vsim-resume modes**: `bash demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` and `bash demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` each exit 0 with a "round-trip complete" banner.
5. **Orchestrator modes**: `bash demo_archive_replay.sh` creates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` and replays exit 0.
6. **Centerpiece**: `bash demo_simpoint_loop.sh` runs the full M4->M5->M6->M7 chain; K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. Speedup line printed.
7. **All modes**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` runs T1..T7 sequentially, prints a final tally `7/7 PASS` in under 5 minutes.
8. **CTest integration**: `ctest -L switching-mode --output-on-failure` from the host build dir runs the same set under CTest's harness; each mode shows up as a labeled test.
9. **Skip behavior**: on a fresh container without `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`, T4 and T7 skip (return 77); CTest reports `Skipped` not `Failed`.
10. **Regression detection**: deliberately break the BBV emitter (e.g., off-by-one in slice boundary) and verify T4 fails with `weights do not sum to 1.0` and T7 fails with `simpts file empty`. Deliberately break F10 (e.g., skip `validate_replay_meta`) and verify T6 fails with `archive validation rejected`.

---

## Out of Scope

- **Phase 4 Kanata-loop integration** (M9-equivalent): no test added; depends on `--kanata-loop-start/end` orchestrator plumbing that doesn't exist yet. Documented as the next mode to add when Phase 4 lands.
- **Multi-hart switching**: `_NDS_NHART <= 2` is supported by the wire struct but the resume/drain path is only exercised on hart 0; per `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` L6.
- **Performance assertion in T7**: the speedup line is informational. A fully-RTL baseline run for the same icount window is a separate work item (Phase 4 verification).
- **State-coverage expansion** (FP/M-CSR/PMP round-trip): orthogonal to switching modes; tracked elsewhere as Phase 5 hardening.
- **CTest parallel scheduling for the demo set**: each demo runs sequentially under the runner; `ctest -j` can still schedule them concurrently but the `make demo` path stays linear so the banner output is readable.

input.plan:
# Comprehensive E2E Test Plan: Switching-Mode Demonstration

## Context

The hybrid simulator now ships **all eight switching mechanisms** (F1-F12) that select when QEMU yields to vsim and vice versa, but the test surface treats them as isolated plugin checks. There is no single artifact a new user (or contributor) can run that demonstrates every switching mode end-to-end, and the SimPoint-driven flow - the highest-leverage mode for production benchmarking - has no integrated test that chains BBV emit -> SimPoint clustering -> phase replay.

This plan adds:
1. One **demonstration e2e fixture per switching mode**, each named after the mode it shows off, runnable via a single `make demo` target.
2. The **SimPoint integrated loop** (currently the only F-feature without a chain-of-modes test): BBV profile -> simpoint binary -> archive each chosen phase -> replay each phase under vsim.
3. A **mode-matrix CTest section** in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` so every switching mode has a labeled CTest entry and shows up under `ctest -L switching-mode`.

Intended outcome: a user can run `make demo` and watch QEMU<->vsim swap drivers eight different ways in under five minutes; a contributor breaking any switching mode gets a labeled CTest failure that names the mode.

---

## Switching Mode Catalog (eight modes, today)

| # | Mode | Trigger source | Today's test |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | **CSR insn match** | `csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1}` in binary | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=csr) |
| M2 | **PC symbol resolve** | `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` ELF symbols | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=pc) |
| M3 | **Icount** (F5/F6) | plugin `icount=N`, vsim `--hybrid-icount N` | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M4 | **BBV-emit** (F7) | plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N` | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` |
| M5 | **Slice-consume** (F11) | plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M6 | **Archive emit** (F9) | orchestrator YAML `on_exit.archive_as` | unit test only |
| M7 | **Checkpoint replay** (F10) | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | unit test only |
| M8 | **End-of-program** | QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit | implicit in every Tier-2 e2e |

Coverage gaps (the targets of this plan):
- **M3** has no vsim-side e2e (vsim receives plugin-drained state and resumes).
- **M5** has no orchestrator-level e2e (drain at slice K -> resume under vsim -> finish).
- **M6+M7** have no e2e at all - both are unit-tested in isolation; never wired through a real round-trip.
- **No fixture chains M4 -> simpoint binary -> M5 -> M6 -> M7** (the SimPoint integrated loop).

---

## New Tests to Add (one per mode)

All seven new e2e drivers live under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` and reuse the existing `icount_spin.elf` workload (CSR-less spin at 0x100000), `rt_c_v_regs.elf` for state-rich modes, plus the orchestrator CLI. Each driver writes its own short log preamble naming the mode it exercises.

### T1. `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (covers M1, M8)
Re-thin wrapper over `roundtrip_e2e.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Justification: a single self-documenting script that prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1)" before invoking the harness. No new code, just a labeled entrypoint for the demo runner.

### T2. `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (covers M2)
Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path runs end-to-end.

### T3. `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M3 end-to-end)
- Drives `icount_spin.elf` through QEMU plugin with `icount=10000`.
- Plugin drains at insn 10000 -> exit 200.
- Orchestrator hands state to vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` (vsim drains 5k cycles later).
- vsim exits 200; spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub; QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit.
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0, drained PC in `icount_spin` body both times.
- Why new: today only `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` exists, and it terminates after QEMU1 drain. The vsim resume path under icount mode is untested at e2e level.

### T4. `demo_bbv_profile.sh` (covers M4 + tool integration)
- Generalize the existing `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` so it leaves the `.bb` file in `$LOG_DIR/bbv/icount_spin.bb` instead of `mktemp`.
- Run `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile bbv/icount_spin.bb -maxK 8 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights weights`.
- Assert the output files exist, parse them, print the chosen slice indices + weights as the demo output.
- Pass criterion: simpoint binary exits 0, weights sum to 1.0 (+/-1e-3).
- No vsim involved; this is the QEMU-only profiling pass.

### T5. `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M5 end-to-end + M8)
- Read the `simpts` file from T4's output (or accept `SLICE_AT=K` env override).
- Drive `icount_spin.elf` with `slice=1000,slice_at=K,icount=<large fallback>`.
- Plugin drains exactly at insn (K+1) * 1000.
- vsim resumes, runs ROI, drains via CSR or icount (configurable), QEMU2 finishes.
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body, QEMU2 exit 0.
- Why new: today `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` is QEMU-only.

### T6. `demo_archive_replay.sh` (NEW; covers M6 + M7 end-to-end)
- Step 1: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root $LOG_DIR/snap --workdir $LOG_DIR/run1`
  - Asserts `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist.
  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.

### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:

```
1. Profile pass (M4)
   $ qemu+plugin icount_spin.elf bbv_out=bbv.bb slice=1000 icount=10000
   -> 10-row bbv.bb

2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)
   $ simpoint -loadFVFile bbv.bb -maxK 4 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights w
   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights

3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
     YAML:
       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

4. For each archive: (M7)
   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
```

Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:
- Each phase archive validates.
- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.

Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).

### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo      # alias for ./demo.sh
```

---

## Mode-Matrix CTest Section

Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:

```cmake
# Switching-mode demo set. Each test asserts one of the eight modes
# (M1..M8) end-to-end. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
# the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`.
set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
  "demo_csr_oneshot:M1+M8:CSR insn match -> end-of-program"
  "demo_pc_oneshot:M2:PC symbol resolve"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip:M3:Icount trigger E2E"
  "demo_bbv_profile:M4:BBV emit + SimPoint clustering"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:M5:Slice-consume drain E2E"
  "demo_archive_replay:M6+M7:Checkpoint archive + replay"
  "demo_simpoint_loop:M4->M5->M6->M7:Full SimPoint pipeline"
)
foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 mode)
  list(GET parts 2 desc)
  add_test(NAME test_${fname}
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${fname}.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
    COST 60)
endforeach()
```

Each test honors `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` so missing simpoint binary / missing plugin / missing ELFs skip cleanly rather than failing the suite on a fresh checkout.

---

  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.

### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:

```
1. Profile pass (M4)
   $ qemu+plugin icount_spin.elf bbv_out=bbv.bb slice=1000 icount=10000
   -> 10-row bbv.bb

2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)
   $ simpoint -loadFVFile bbv.bb -maxK 4 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights w
   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights

3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
     YAML:
       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

4. For each archive: (M7)
   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
```

Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:
- Each phase archive validates.
- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.

Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).

### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.

```sh
make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo      # alias for ./demo.sh
```

---

## Mode-Matrix CTest Section

Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:

```cmake
# Switching-mode demo set. Each test asserts one of the eight modes
# (M1..M8) end-to-end. Labeled "switching-mode" so contributors can run
# the subset with `ctest -L switching-mode`.
set(VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS
  "demo_csr_oneshot:M1+M8:CSR insn match -> end-of-program"
  "demo_pc_oneshot:M2:PC symbol resolve"
  "demo_icount_roundtrip:M3:Icount trigger E2E"
  "demo_bbv_profile:M4:BBV emit + SimPoint clustering"
  "demo_slice_consume_roundtrip:M5:Slice-consume drain E2E"
  "demo_archive_replay:M6+M7:Checkpoint archive + replay"
  "demo_simpoint_loop:M4->M5->M6->M7:Full SimPoint pipeline"
)
foreach(entry ${VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS})
  string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
  list(GET parts 0 fname)
  list(GET parts 1 mode)
  list(GET parts 2 desc)
  add_test(NAME test_${fname}
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/${fname}.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_${fname} PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e;switching-mode"
    COST 60)
endforeach()
```

Each test honors `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` so missing simpoint binary / missing plugin / missing ELFs skip cleanly rather than failing the suite on a fresh checkout.

---

## Critical Files

To create (no existing implementation):
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh` - user-facing runner
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` - parameterized SimPoint phase YAML used by T7

To modify:
- `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` - add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach (see above)
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - new `demo` phony target invoking `demo.sh`
- `run_e2e.sh` - extend `RT_TESTS` with the seven demo entries so the host runner picks them up (single `MODE=demo` knob optional)

To reuse without modification:
- `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - already supports every plugin-side switching arg
- `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint` - F12 clustering binary
- `tools/orchestrator/main.py` + `tools/orchestrator/examples/csr_oneshot.yaml` - YAML driver and example
- `tools/orchestrator/checkpoint.py` (`write_archive`, `replay_checkpoint`, `validate_replay_meta`) - F9+F10 entry points
- `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/test_plugin_{bbv_emit,phase_consume,icount_trigger}.sh` - reference implementations for plugin invocation patterns
- `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/{icount_spin.S,rt_c_v_regs.c}` - workloads
- `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/{handoff_controller,resume_driver,state_drain,qemu_handback}.hpp` - the entire vsim-side switching mechanism

---

## Verification

End-to-end ladder, each step requires the previous:

1. **Build**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` builds all referenced ELFs (already exist; rule is just an alias for `make demo.sh`).
2. **Single-mode**: `bash hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` exits 0 and prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1) - PASS".
3. **Plugin-only modes**: `bash demo_bbv_profile.sh` produces a `.bb` file, runs simpoint, prints chosen slices + weights.
4. **Vsim-resume modes**: `bash demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` and `bash demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` each exit 0 with a "round-trip complete" banner.
5. **Orchestrator modes**: `bash demo_archive_replay.sh` creates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` and replays exit 0.
6. **Centerpiece**: `bash demo_simpoint_loop.sh` runs the full M4->M5->M6->M7 chain; K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. Speedup line printed.
7. **All modes**: `make -C hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test demo` runs T1..T7 sequentially, prints a final tally `7/7 PASS` in under 5 minutes.
8. **CTest integration**: `ctest -L switching-mode --output-on-failure` from the host build dir runs the same set under CTest's harness; each mode shows up as a labeled test.
9. **Skip behavior**: on a fresh container without `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`, T4 and T7 skip (return 77); CTest reports `Skipped` not `Failed`.
10. **Regression detection**: deliberately break the BBV emitter (e.g., off-by-one in slice boundary) and verify T4 fails with `weights do not sum to 1.0` and T7 fails with `simpts file empty`. Deliberately break F10 (e.g., skip `validate_replay_meta`) and verify T6 fails with `archive validation rejected`.

- **Phase 4 Kanata-loop integration** (M9-equivalent): no test added; depends on `--kanata-loop-start/end` orchestrator plumbing that doesn't exist yet. Documented as the next mode to add when Phase 4 lands.
- **Multi-hart switching**: `_NDS_NHART <= 2` is supported by the wire struct but the resume/drain path is only exercised on hart 0; per `HYBRID_OVERVIEW.md` L6.
- **Performance assertion in T7**: the speedup line is informational. A fully-RTL baseline run for the same icount window is a separate work item (Phase 4 verification).
- **State-coverage expansion** (FP/M-CSR/PMP round-trip): orthogonal to switching modes; tracked elsewhere as Phase 5 hardening.
- **CTest parallel scheduling for the demo set**: each demo runs sequentially under the runner; `ctest -j` can still schedule them concurrently but the `make demo` path stays linear so the banner output is readable.

toolUseResult.plan:
# Comprehensive E2E Test Plan: Switching-Mode Demonstration

## Context

The hybrid simulator now ships **all eight switching mechanisms** (F1-F12) that select when QEMU yields to vsim and vice versa, but the test surface treats them as isolated plugin checks. There is no single artifact a new user (or contributor) can run that demonstrates every switching mode end-to-end, and the SimPoint-driven flow - the highest-leverage mode for production benchmarking - has no integrated test that chains BBV emit -> SimPoint clustering -> phase replay.

This plan adds:
1. One **demonstration e2e fixture per switching mode**, each named after the mode it shows off, runnable via a single `make demo` target.
2. The **SimPoint integrated loop** (currently the only F-feature without a chain-of-modes test): BBV profile -> simpoint binary -> archive each chosen phase -> replay each phase under vsim.
3. A **mode-matrix CTest section** in `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` so every switching mode has a labeled CTest entry and shows up under `ctest -L switching-mode`.

Intended outcome: a user can run `make demo` and watch QEMU<->vsim swap drivers eight different ways in under five minutes; a contributor breaking any switching mode gets a labeled CTest failure that names the mode.

---

## Switching Mode Catalog (eight modes, today)

| # | Mode | Trigger source | Today's test |
|---|---|---|---|
| M1 | **CSR insn match** | `csrwi 0x7C0, {0,1}` in binary | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=csr) |
| M2 | **PC symbol resolve** | `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc` ELF symbols | `roundtrip_e2e.sh` (TRIGGER_MODE=pc) |
| M3 | **Icount** (F5/F6) | plugin `icount=N`, vsim `--hybrid-icount N` | `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M4 | **BBV-emit** (F7) | plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N` | `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` |
| M5 | **Slice-consume** (F11) | plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` | `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` (QEMU only) |
| M6 | **Archive emit** (F9) | orchestrator YAML `on_exit.archive_as` | unit test only |
| M7 | **Checkpoint replay** (F10) | `orchestrator replay-checkpoint <dir>` | unit test only |
| M8 | **End-of-program** | QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit | implicit in every Tier-2 e2e |

Coverage gaps (the targets of this plan):
- **M3** has no vsim-side e2e (vsim receives plugin-drained state and resumes).
- **M5** has no orchestrator-level e2e (drain at slice K -> resume under vsim -> finish).
- **M6+M7** have no e2e at all - both are unit-tested in isolation; never wired through a real round-trip.
- **No fixture chains M4 -> simpoint binary -> M5 -> M6 -> M7** (the SimPoint integrated loop).

---

## New Tests to Add (one per mode)

All seven new e2e drivers live under `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/` and reuse the existing `icount_spin.elf` workload (CSR-less spin at 0x100000), `rt_c_v_regs.elf` for state-rich modes, plus the orchestrator CLI. Each driver writes its own short log preamble naming the mode it exercises.

### T1. `demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (covers M1, M8)
Re-thin wrapper over `roundtrip_e2e.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Justification: a single self-documenting script that prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1)" before invoking the harness. No new code, just a labeled entrypoint for the demo runner.

### T2. `demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (covers M2)
Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path runs end-to-end.

### T3. `demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M3 end-to-end)
- Drives `icount_spin.elf` through QEMU plugin with `icount=10000`.
- Plugin drains at insn 10000 -> exit 200.
- Orchestrator hands state to vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` (vsim drains 5k cycles later).
- vsim exits 200; spawns QEMU2 via gdbstub; QEMU2 runs to semihosting exit.
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0, drained PC in `icount_spin` body both times.
- Why new: today only `test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh` exists, and it terminates after QEMU1 drain. The vsim resume path under icount mode is untested at e2e level.

### T4. `demo_bbv_profile.sh` (covers M4 + tool integration)
- Generalize the existing `test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh` so it leaves the `.bb` file in `$LOG_DIR/bbv/icount_spin.bb` instead of `mktemp`.
- Run `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile bbv/icount_spin.bb -maxK 8 -saveSimpoints simpts -saveSimpointWeights weights`.
- Assert the output files exist, parse them, print the chosen slice indices + weights as the demo output.
- Pass criterion: simpoint binary exits 0, weights sum to 1.0 (+/-1e-3).
- No vsim involved; this is the QEMU-only profiling pass.

### T5. `demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (NEW; covers M5 end-to-end + M8)
- Read the `simpts` file from T4's output (or accept `SLICE_AT=K` env override).
- Drive `icount_spin.elf` with `slice=1000,slice_at=K,icount=<large fallback>`.
- Plugin drains exactly at insn (K+1) * 1000.
- vsim resumes, runs ROI, drains via CSR or icount (configurable), QEMU2 finishes.
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body, QEMU2 exit 0.
- Why new: today `test_plugin_phase_consume.sh` is QEMU-only.

### T6. `demo_archive_replay.sh` (NEW; covers M6 + M7 end-to-end)

  - Asserts `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist.
  - Validates `meta.json` schema=1, abi_magic matches, mem_size matches mmap.
- Step 2: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint $LOG_DIR/snap/0 --resume-with vsim --workdir $LOG_DIR/run2`
  - Asserts replay session exits 0.
  - Asserts post-replay state.bin differs from `snap/0/state.bin` (PC advanced).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; archive shape + replay validation match.
- Why new: F9/F10 exist only as unit tests today; no test wires both through an actual mmap + state file.

### T7. `demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (NEW; the centerpiece, covers M4 -> simpoint -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)
The full SimPoint-driven workflow, the production use case for hybrid sim:

```

2. Cluster (tools/simpoint)

   -> simpts: list of (slice_idx, cluster_id); w: cluster weights

3. For each chosen slice_idx in simpts: (M5 + M6)
   $ orchestrator run plan_phase_${idx}.yaml --archive-root snap --workdir run_${idx}
     YAML:
       step 1: qemu with slice=1000,slice_at=${idx} -> drain
       on_exit: archive_as: snap/${idx}
   -> snap/${idx}/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}

4. For each archive: (M7)
   $ orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/${idx} --resume-with vsim --workdir replay_${idx}
   -> vsim resumes from cluster-representative state, runs ROI, optional QEMU2 finish
```

Driver script generates the per-phase YAMLs from a template, invokes simpoint, walks both loops, and asserts:

- Each replay exits 0 (or a documented non-zero with a clear reason for end-of-program phases).
- Final wall-clock printout: `phases_replayed=K, total_vsim_cycles=N, baseline_cycles=M, speedup=M/N`.

Pass criterion: K phase archives created, K replays exit 0. The speedup line is informational, not asserted (deterministic only on `icount_spin`).

### Demo runner: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh`
A single user-facing entry point. Sequentially invokes T1..T7, printing a banner per mode and a final pass/fail tally. Honors `FILTER=<mode-name>` env to skip subsets. Runs in under five minutes on the CI host when the build is cached.

```sh

```

---

## Mode-Matrix CTest Section

Add to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` after the existing plugin tests:

content.text: Plan approved. Following the project's "one feature per session" rule, I'll start with T1 (the simplest demo wrapper) after creating a ROADMAP for tracking. First let me check build artifacts and the harness interface.

content.content:
/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64
/home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# End-to-end roundtrip:
3	#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
4	#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
5	#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
6	#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
7	#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
8	#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
9	#
10	# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
11	#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
12	#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
13	#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
14	#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
15	#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
16	#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
17	#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
18	#
19	# Fixture-parameterized:
20	#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
21	#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
22	#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
23	#
24	# Required environment:
25	#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
26	#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
27	#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
28	#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
29	#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)
30	
31	set -euo pipefail
32	
33	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
34	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
35	
36	FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
37	EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
38	TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"
39	
40	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
41	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
42	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state on entry trigger.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains on exit trigger, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Two trigger modes (TRIGGER_MODE env, default csr):
#   csr  - QEMU plugin pattern-matches `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` to drain on entry;
#          vsim pre-scans the ELF and drains on `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` retire.
#   pc   - orchestrator resolves _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the
#          ELF symbol table, passes enter_pc=0x... to the plugin and
#          --hybrid-exit-pc=0x... to vsim. The fixture's csrwi insns are
#          still in the binary (CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor CSR write on
#          andes-ax45mp + the AX45MPV RTL) but the matchers are bypassed.
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE       name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC     expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#   TRIGGER_MODE  csr | pc                          (default: csr)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)
#   NM               riscv64-unknown-elf-nm  (default auto-detect)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')
    EXIT_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
              | awk '$3=="_hybrid_exit_pc"{print $1}')
    if [ -z "${ENTER_PC:-}" ] || [ -z "${EXIT_PC:-}" ]; then
      echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE pc]: missing _hybrid_enter_pc/_hybrid_exit_pc symbols"
      exit 1
    fi
    ;;
  *)
    echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: unknown TRIGGER_MODE=$TRIGGER_MODE (use csr|pc)"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)

Tracks 12 independent features to extend hybrid_sim (QEMU + vsim) with the four
CPU-switch mechanisms from `specs/hybrid-sim-gem5-switch-parity.md`. One
session = one feature. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: feature has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session

```
F1 ----+----> F4
F2 ----+
F3 ----+
F5 ----+----> F6
       +----> F7 ----> F12 ----> F11
       +----> F8 ----> F9 ----> F10

Migration order: ship F1, F2, F3 first (infrastructure layer). After that,
F5/F6/F7 parallelise across sessions because they touch independent files.
F12 (SimPoint binary integration) sits between BBV emission (F7) and
slice-consume (F11) because F11's slice indices come from SimPoint's
clustering output.

## Features

### F1 - Rebrand plugin drain-exit code 0 -> 200

- Status: done
- Type: structural
- Description: Today `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:261` exits 0 on drain;
  workload completion and drain are indistinguishable. vsim already exits
  200 (`hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/exit_codes.hpp:7`). Bring the plugin in
  line. The orchestrator (F2+) relies on this distinction.

  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` (exit-code assertions)
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh`
- Dependencies: none
- TDD RED: a test in `tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh` runs
  the plugin with a CSR fixture and asserts QEMU exits with code 200.
  Fails on today's 0.

### F2 - Orchestrator skeleton (Python package)

file.content:
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

PLUGIN_ARGS="outfile=$STATE_QEMU"
VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512:
#   - andes-ax45mpv gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl,
#     mxstatus, uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that the vsim-demo crt0/handler
#     touch; default rv64 illegal-traps on the first mcache_ctl write.
#   - vlen=512 pins QEMU's VLEN to 512 bits (vlenb=64), matching the vsim
#     RTL build (data/configs/ax45mpv_premium.cfg: NDS_VLEN 512) and
#     HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX=64 in include/hybrid/state_abi.h. The default
#     andes-ax45mpv has VLEN=1024 which silently truncates V-state in
#     the wire format. Non-V fixtures run identically under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.
set +e
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 -bios none -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

# Fixture-parameterized: defaults match the original rt_shared_mem.S/elf
# fixture, but C-level Tier-2 fixtures (rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, ...)
# reuse this harness by overriding FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC.
FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"
TIMEOUT="${TIMEOUT:-60}"
TRIGGER_MODE="${TRIGGER_MODE:-csr}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"
NM="${NM:-/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-nm}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

case "$TRIGGER_MODE" in
  csr) ;;
  pc)
    if ! command -v "$NM" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      echo "skip[$FIXTURE pc]: nm not found at $NM"
      exit 77
    fi
    # Capture nm output once: avoids racing awk's `exit` against nm's pipe
    # writes (large C-fixture symbol tables make nm SIGPIPE under pipefail).
    NM_OUT=$("$NM" --defined-only "$VSIM_ELF")
    ENTER_PC=$(printf '%s\n' "$NM_OUT" \
               | awk '$3=="_hybrid_enter_pc"{print $1}')

# ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: in-progress
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T1: CSR insn-match switching mode (M1) + end-of-program (M8).
#
# Walks one round-trip with CSR triggers (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) on a
# state-rich Tier-2 fixture (rt_c_v_regs). Demonstrates the default
# switching path a user gets out of the box.
#
# Honors all the same env overrides as rt_shared_mem.sh (VSIM_BIN,
# VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM, TIMEOUT).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="CSR insn match (M1+M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 (enter) -> csrwi 0x7C0, 1 (exit)"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M1] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"
